items.claim: Robert Whittaker will be stronger at light heavyweight because he does not have to cut weight.
items.quote: He's going to be stronger. He's taking some time off here.

items.claim: Robert Whittaker will be rejuvenated by moving up to the light heavyweight division.
items.quote: I think Whitaker is going to be rejuvenated.

items.claim: Robert Whittaker will have a speed advantage over Krillov at light heavyweight.
items.quote: he'll have more speed than Krillov I believe here in the light heavyweight division

items.claim: Terrence McKini has a significant advantage in the grappling department.
items.quote: I think Terrence McKini is going to land takedowns at some point and utilize the ground and pound. That's his weapon.

items.claim: Terrence McKini has not been out of the first round in his last seven fights.
items.quote: this guy hasn't been out of the first round in man about seven fights.

items.claim: Brandon Royval has a poor takedown defense percentage of 45%.
items.quote: Roy Val's got a 45% takedown defense.

items.claim: Loner Kavanagh has a high takedown defense rate of 94%.
items.quote: Kavanaaugh 94% takedown defense. That's impressive so far.

items.claim: Cory Sandhagen's takedown defense percentage dropped to 56% after the Merab Dvalishvili fight.
items.quote: all of a sudden, Morab Devali came in and took him down 20 times. So, it dropped that thing way back down to 56%.

items.claim: Mario Bautista has a slight edge in takedown volume over Cory Sandhagen.
items.quote: Bautista nearly two takedowns over three rounds. Sanhagen gets one every three. So, you know, slight edge for Bautista

items.claim: Benois Saint-Denis has a significant advantage in takedown volume over Paddy Pimblett.
items.quote: the takedown game goes to Saint Deni who lands like a takedown and a half per round.

items.claim: Paddy Pimblett has a poor takedown defense rate of 44%.
items.quote: So the take down defense of Patty, the batty's important here, right? 44%.
